data.title: Bank of England Expected to Maintain Interest Rates Amid Economic Challenges
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the Bank of England is anticipated to keep interest rates steady this week, aligning with the U.S. Federal Reserve's recent actions. However, it may implement more significant rate cuts later this year compared to the Federal Reserve. Similar to the U.S. central bank, the Bank of England expects inflation to continue easing in the coming months but aims to ensure that inflation reduction is on track and to avoid lowering borrowing costs too rapidly.

The British economy has been notably weaker than that of the United States. After stagnating in the third quarter, the UK economy grew by only 0.1% in the fourth quarter, narrowly avoiding a recession. Concerns over the impact of the Russia-Ukraine conflict and tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump on global economic growth have affected the UK, which also faces its own economic challenges. The government, led by Prime Minister Keir Starmer, has been striving to restore consumer and business confidence in the economic outlook.

The Bank of England has stated it is adopting a "gradual and cautious approach" to lowering interest rates. However, with the economy stagnating, the direction of interest rates is clear. Economists at Nomura Securities, led by George Buckley, predict that the central bank will maintain rates in March and cut them again in May. They foresee rate cuts at every meeting from now until February 2026, with a terminal rate of 3.5%.

data.title: A smart money address liquidated MUBARAK and made a profit of $517,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to The Data Nerd, a smart money address sold his last 1.94 million MUBARAKs for $229,000. Six days ago, he bought 8.13 million MUBARAKs for $9,600, and then sold them all for $526,000, with an actual profit of $517,000 and a return on investment of 54 times.

data.title: He Yi: We will clean up the cheating behavior in voting and listing coins through vote washing to ensure the fairness of voting
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Regarding the vote-rigging behavior in Binance's voting to list coins, Binance co-founder He Yi commented that they would ensure the fairness of the vote by clearing out cheating behaviors.

data.title: Bitcoin volatility rose to 3.62%, having risen for 14 consecutive trading days
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Coinglass data, Bitcoin volatility has risen for 14 consecutive trading days recently. Today's volatility is 3.62%, reaching its highest point since August last year.

data.title: Bitcoin Volatility Reaches Highest Level Since Last August
data.description: According to BlockBeats, recent data from Coinglass indicates that Bitcoin's volatility has increased for 14 consecutive trading days, reaching 3.62% today. This marks the highest level since August of last year.

data.title: BitMart will launch TUT perpetual contract with a maximum leverage of 50 times
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the official announcement, BitMart will launch the Tutorial (TUT) perpetual contract at 18:00 (UTC+8) on March 20, 2025, and will open the TUT/USDT trading pair with a maximum leverage of 50 times. Tutorial (TUT) is a Meme coin deployed on BNB Chain.

data.title: Dubai Land Department begins real estate tokenization project
data.description:
The Dubai government started the pilot phase of a project that would convert real estate assets into digital tokens on the blockchain. 
The Dubai Land Department (DLD), a government entity responsible for registering, organizing and promoting Dubai real estate, announced that it started the pilot phase of its real-estate tokenization project. 
The project was launched in collaboration with the Dubai Future Foundation (DFF) and the Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA), Dubai’s crypto regulator. 
The token launch makes the DLD the first real-estate registration entity in the UAE to implement tokenization on property title deeds
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/dubai-launches-pilot-tokenize-real-estate-blockchain?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Dubai Land Department announces pilot of real estate tokenization project to tokenize real estate to simplify transactions
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Dubai Land Department DLD has begun implementing a pilot real estate tokenization project. The project is a collaboration between Dubai Future Foundation (DFF) and Dubai's cryptocurrency regulator Virtual Asset Authority (VARA). The authority expects the market value of tokenized real estate to exceed $16 billion by 2033, which, according to the agency, is equivalent to 7% of Dubai's total real estate transactions.
Marwan Ahmed Bin Ghalita, Director General of DLD, said, "By converting real estate assets into digital tokens recorded on blockchain technology, tokenization simplifies and enhances the buying, selling and investment processes," (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Barclays: Fed's assumption of 'temporary' tariff pressure may catch it off guard
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News - Barclays economists said in a report that Fed Chairman Powell's "temporary" remarks on tariff-related price pressures and his disregard for inflation expectations risk catching the Fed off guard, just as it did during the epidemic. Powell made it clear on Wednesday that the committee assumes that tariff-related price pressures will be temporary, although it is taking a "wait-and-see" approach to whether price pressures will play out in this way. "His tone seemed overconfident," Barclays said. The bank added that Powell was also reluctant to consider taking precautionary interest rate cuts to provide a buffer for the economy in the event of a severe deterioration in economic activity. This was foreshadowed by weak data indicators such as consumer confidence. They added that downplaying such risks could undermine the Fed's credibility, as the Fed appears to be on a very narrow path with double risks at every step. (Jinshi)
